metadata.description: Composite hip and knee arthroplasty model linking joint surfaces, soft tissues, and implant interfaces.

Bearing insert articulating with femoral head or femoral component.

Highly cross-linked PE reducing volumetric wear in arthroplasty.
Low-wear ceramic couple with fracture and squeak considerations.
TKA polyethylene bearing surface articulating with femoral component.
Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Posterior hip soft tissue sleeve repaired after posterior THA approach.
Thigh bone bearing femoral condyles and canal for arthroplasty stem fixation.
Articular femoral head supplied largely by retinacular and circumflex vessels.
Fibrocartilaginous hip rim contributing to suction seal and joint stability.
Cementless implant stability achieved through interference fit and bone ingrowth.

Ball-and-socket articulation between femoral head and acetabulum.
Distal femoral articular surfaces resurfaced during TKA femoral component placement.
Proximal extensor mechanism linkage at risk in TKA exposure and extensor failure.

Resurfaced patellar button in select TKA and patellofemoral arthroplasty cases.

Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.

Deep hip abductor contributing to pelvic stability during gait.
Anterior hip nerve at risk during anterior approach THA exposure.
Intracapsular segment between femoral head and intertrochanteric region.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.

TKA femoral resurfacing component restoring flexion-extension geometry.
TKA tibial tray fixed to proximal tibia receiving polyethylene insert.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
PMMA cement securing implant to bone for immediate stability.
Posterior hip nerve at risk during proximal femur exposure and reduction maneuvers.
THA femoral canal implant transmitting load from head to diaphysis.
